body.title: Constraint normalizer: build devlab.constraints ingestion pipeline
body.result: CC-validated (commit 6beeb169). ingest() wires live palace via _parse_palace_rules_db → 3 sources (claude_md/design_patterns/palace=49). AR-009 log-crossings classified hard_block (narrow). get_constraints exposed as MCP tools (devices/hubert/tools.py). All 3 completion criteria verified vs live DB; 6 tests pass. NOTE: _parse_palace_rules_db reads clan.memory_palace via direct SQL — at the D-filesystem-memory-store cutover this parser must repoint to the JSON store or it silently drops to 2 sources. Link to T-decisions-dsb-cutover / filesystem-memory cutover work.
body.intention: I intend that devlab.constraints.* is a queryable, up-to-date store of normalized constraints ingested from all constraint sources, callable by the decoration pipeline at ticket-add time.
body.description:
Constraints live in CLAUDE.md, palace rules, design_patterns_inventory.md, and the safeguards list — in incompatible formats. The normalizer ingests these sources and produces a uniform queryable schema in devlab.constraints.

Normalized schema per record:
  id, text, kind (prohibit|require|prefer), severity (hard_block|error|warn),
  applies_to jsonb {files: [...], operations: [...], tags: [...]},
  source jsonb {type, ref}, implies jsonb[] (IDs of implied constraints),
  created_at, updated_at

V1 ingestion sources:
  1. CLAUDE.md hard rules section
  2. palace unseenuniversity/rules/* nodes (via MCP memory_get)
  3. docs/design_patterns_inventory.md
  4. Safeguards HIGH-inertia list

Exposes MCP tool: get_constraints(files=[], tags=[], severity=None) -> list of matching constraints.
Called by the ticket decorator (T-constraint-normalizer-decorator), NOT by sprint agents directly.

**Affected files:** devices/hubert/constraint_normalizer.py (new); MCP tool registration in devices/hubert/; SQL migration devlab.constraints (covered by T-devlab-schema-create)
**Design rules:** external-state; all queries via MCP bus; no SQLite
**Scope boundary:** IN — ingest pipeline + devlab.constraints store + get_constraints MCP tool. OUT — calling it at ticket time (T-constraint-normalizer-decorator); using it in sprint agents (they see pre-stamped ticket).
**Completion criteria:** get_constraints(files=["devices/inference/sources.py"]) returns >=4 constraints from >=3 sources; hard_block constraints include no-sqlite and log-crossings; re-ingestion is idempotent (run twice, same row count).
